title: Xiaomi 17 Pro Max vs iPhone 17 Pro Max Comparison Review
transcript: So, I've had these two smartphones, the Xiaomi 17 Pro Max and the iPhone 17 Pro Max in my pocket for about a week now, which is typical me, iPhone and Android in my pocket. These devices are so identical that it's actually creepy and confusing. When I try to adjust the volume on the Xiaomi, I most times press on the wrong side because it really does look like the iPhone and feels like an iPhone. These devices feel premium and look expensive except for the Xiaomi which looks futuristic with this rare display panel at the back. You can actually use this to take selfies with the rare cameras and even video recordings. For the displays on the smartphones, you get 3,000 nits of brightness on the iPhone and 3,500 nits on the Xiaomi, the same as the back display. Both are 120 Hz refresh rate. Aside brightness, they look the same for color reproduction except when you go into the settings and boost the colors on the Xiaomi 17 Pro Max. Inside these devices, you're getting the Snapdragon 88 Elite Gen 5 processor on the Xiaomi and the A19 Pro chip on this Apple device. For raw numbers, this is faster, the Xiaomi, but for efficiency, the Apple device is better. Now, speaking of efficiency, you get a 7,500 mAh battery on Xiaomi, 5,88 for the ESIM, which is the better variant on the iPhone. But from my day-to-day use, they're about the same experience, like nothing different. The only reason you pick the Xiaomi in terms of power would be for the 100 watt charger which charges this device from 0 to 100% in 20 minutes which is the same time to get to 50% on the iPhone. For the cameras, you're getting 50 MP sensors for the Xiaomi front cameras and every other camera 50 megap. On the iPhone you get 48 MP for the three cameras at the back and an 80 megapixel selfie camera. Now the photos are really identical. The only time you notice differences between the photos is when you pixel peep and see that Xiaomi lifts the shadows. The Xiaomi has this brighter and punchy vibe for the colors. And even when you turn off the beauty filter, it still does a little bit of smooning. iPhone gives you something more natural like what you'd see. And for the skin tones, I most often than not prefer what I see with the iPhone. But other than that, for general photos, the Xiaomi seems to be a better device, better at zoom, better at the ultra wide, handles sunlight better, and gives you this more punchy colors. Where the iPhone wins would be in the front-facing camera. It does better. But when you switch over to the back camera and use this display as your viewfinder to take your selfie, this smartphone has the best selfie camera on any smartphone right now. The only reason I'd want to go with the iPhone in terms of the camera would be video. Now, you can shoot in ProRes RAW or log format on the smartphone, which is not available on the Xiaomi, and the skin tones tend to be better for video on the iPhone. So, here's the thing. If you shoot a lot of videos and you're stuck in the Apple ecosystem, then the iPhone 17 Pro Max might be the device for you. But if you want a better hardware, you want dual displays, you want the better photo camera, you want a faster charging time, the better overall value for money, $8.99 against $1199, $300 difference, then go with the Xiaomi 17 Pro Max. is an all round better

title: Elegance in Air 🍎 iPhone Air சம்பவம்⭐Tech Superstar⭐
transcript: அதாவது பாத்தீங்கன்னா iPhoneன் யூசர்னாலே நிறைய  விஷயங்களை சாக்ரிபைஸ் பண்ணிதான் அந்த போன  யூஸ் பண்றாங்கன்னு ஆண்ட்ராய்டு யூசர்ஸ் மத்தியில பெரிதளவும் பேசப்படுது.  ஆனா ஒரு iPhoneன் யூசரே இந்த போனை யூஸ் பண்ணனும்னா இன்னும் சில விஷயங்களை சாக்ரிபைஸ் பண்ற மாதிரிதான் இருக்குது.  அப்படி ஒரு போனை பத்திதான் இன்னைக்கு நம்ம வீடியோல பாக்க போறோம்.  iPhone கா. உலகத்துலேயே iPhone யாருக்கு இந்த மாதிரி ஒரு இன்ட்ரோ நீங்க பார்த்திருக்கவே மாட்டீங்க.  எனக்கு புரியாத விஷயம் என்ன அப்படின்னா, எதுக்கு இந்த வன்மோ அப்படி என்ன உங்களுக்கு  அந்த போன்ல ஆச்சு.  அதாவது பாத்தீங்கன்னா நான் ஒரு ஏய்  வேண்டாப்பா சாமி நீ உன் ஸ்டைல்ல பேசு. இல்ல தலைவா நான் ஒரு iPhoneன் 13 யூசர். எடுத்தெடுப்புலயே என்கிட்டன் யார தள்ளி  விட்டீங்க அப்படின்னா செம்ம அப்கிரேடா  ஃபீல் ஆகும்ல ஃபீல் ஆகும்.  ஆனா எனக்கும் சில விஷயங்கள் குறையா தெரியுதுல்ல?   How to use iPhone 13 எங்க இருக்கு? iPhone 6 எங்க இருக்கு?  எவ்வளவு பெரிய அப்கிரேட் ஜம்ப் அப்ப அதுக்குஏத்தாலும் நல்ல விதத்துல தானேயா உனக்கு இருந்திருக்கணும். நிறைய விஷயங்கள் நல்ல விஷயங்களா எனக்கு தெரியும்.  ஆனா ₹ லட்ச2,000 போட்டு வாங்குறவனுக்கு நிறைய விஷயங்கள் குறைபாடா தெரியும்ல அதத்தான் நான் சொல்ல  வரேன். சரி அப்படி என்ன சாக்ரிபைஸ் பண்ணீங்க சொல்லுங்க முதல்ல ₹ல2,000 போட்டு இந்த போன  வாங்குனா சிங்கிள் கேமராதான்யா கொடுத்துருக்கேன் ஆமாங்கயா அதுக்கு வேற வழியே கிடையாது.  48 மகபிக்ல் ியூஷன் கேமரா அதே 24 மகபிக்ல்ல  வந்து பிக்சர்ஸ்லாம் எடுக்க முடியும் அப்படில்லாம் சொன்னாலும் இதை ஏத்துக்கவே முடியல பிகாஸ்  ஒரு 16 அந்த மாதிரி ஏதாவது ஒரு வெர்ஷன்லயே ஒரு  கேமரா அப்படின்றப்பயே நம்மளுக்கு கடுப்பாகுது. அப்படின்றப்ப இன்னும் கேமரா வந்து ஏதாவது அந்த ஆட் பண்ணிருந்துருக்கலாம். அதை ஆட் பண்ணாது குறைகள் தாங்க. ஆமாங்க ஒரு ஒயட் ஆங்கிள் கிடையாது ஒரு டெலி  கிடையாது. டெலிக்கு பதிலாதான் 2xல எடுக்கலாம்லங்க. 1x 2xல என்னென்ன எடுப்பீங்க? அன்னைக்கு ஆயுத பூஜைக்கு ஒரு வீடியோ எடுக்குறேங்க.  நான் பின்னாடி போய் எடுக்க வேண்டியதா  இருக்குது. ஒரு ஒயட குடுத்தீங்கன்னா டக்குன்னு போட்டு  டம் டும்மு டம்ன்னு எடுத்துருக்கலாம். கரெக்ட்தான் கரெக்டான பாயிண்ட்.  ஆனா என்னன்னா இது ஸ்லிம் அப்படின்ற  ரீசனுக்காக கேமரா வந்து நிறைய கேமரா  வச்சாங்கன்னா ஸ்லிம்மா உங்களால மெயின்டைன் பண்ண முடியாதுல்ல தலைவா. ஸ்லிம்மா இருக்கறதுனால ஒரு கேமரான்னு  சொல்றீங்க. ஃியூஷன் கேமராதான 48 MP ஃியூஷன் கேமராதானே ஒரு ரா போட்டோ எடுக்க முடியல ரால வீடியோ எடுத்து  போஸ்ட்ல எடிட் பண்ண முடியல. ப்ரோ பீச்சர் வந்து ப்ரோக்கு தான்.  அது வந்து ஆர் அப்ப ப்ரோல கொடுத்த ப்ராசசர் மட்டும் கொடுத்துருக்கோம்னு கூவிக்கிட்டே இருந்தாங்க.   ஏய் ஏய் ஏய் அத அப்படி சொல்லுவாங்க. நல்லா புரிஞ்சுக்கோங்க மக்களே பேர் மட்டும்தான் ஒன்னு. அதுல ஒரு கோர் குறையுது நான் ப்ராசசர் பத்தி  பேசல வரேன்.   அதுக்காகதான் காத்துனுக்கறேன். இந்த போன்ல சினிமேட்டிக் மோடு இருக்கு.  இதுல இல்ல.  ஏன்? நியாயமான கேள்விதான். நீ ஸ்லிம் ஆக்குற அதுக்காக கேமரா வந்து இது  பண்ணல.  சரி. சாப்ட்வேர் ஃீச்சரா கொடுக்குறதுக்கு உனக்கு என்ன நொட்டுதுன்னு தெரியல. ஒரு சினிமேட்டிக் மோட கூட கட் பண்ணி வச்சிருக்காங்க. இல்ல சிங்கிள் கேமரா வச்சு பண்ண முடியாதமோ.  இல்ல தெரியல எனக்கு.  இல்ல உண்மையாவே தெரியலையா? சூப்பரா இருக்குது ஸ்லோ மோஷன் எல்லாமே ஓகேதான்.  பிிரண்ட் கேமராவும் தரமா இருக்குது. போட்டோவும் நல்லா இருக்குது வீடியோவும் நல்லா இருக்குது. ஆமா அந்த 18 மகபிக்ல்ன்றது நாலு போன்லயும் ஒரே கொடுத்துத்திருக்காங்க. சோ சேம் செல்பி கேமரா அவுட்புட் வாஸ் குட் சென்டர் ஸ்டேஜ் கேமரா கொடுத்துருக்கனால வீடியோ  ஸ்டெபிலிட்டி எடுக்கறதுக்கு ஈஸியா இருக்குது. எனக்கு தெரிஞ்சு இனிமே நிறைய பேர் வந்து இதே  மாதிரி கேமரா கொடுத்து லாாகிங் யூஸ் பண்ணுங்கடா அப்படின்னு கொண்டு வரதுக்கு ஒரு ட்ரெண்ட்  இருந்தாலும் ஆச்சரியத்து கிடையாது. டூயல் கேப்சர் மோடு கொடுத்துருக்கேன்.   ிரண்ட் அண்ட் பேக்கையும் சேர்த்து எடுத்துக்கலாம்.  ஆனா ிரண்ட் டு பேக் பேக் டு ிரண்ட் ஸ்விட்ச் பண்ணி எடுத்துக்க முடியாது. அப்படி எடுக்கணும்னா நீங்க Samsung தான் போணும்.  நீங்க வேணா Samsungல வேண போனா சஜஸ்ட் பண்ணவா அதெல்லாம் வேணாங்கன்ல இத கொண்டு வரணுமா இல்லையா அது iOS 27ல வரும்யா வரும். அழுகாத ராசா அழுகாதடோட் பீல் போட்டோஸ்  எப்படி இருந்துச்சு மெயின் கேமரால மெயின்  கேமரால எடுக்கற போட்டோஸ் எல்லாமே பச்சக் பச்சக்  பச்சக்குன்னு எடுக்கும் போதெல்லாம் தாரமா  இருந்துச்சு. கலர்ஸ் நல்லா இருக்குது, டீடைல்ஸ் நல்லா  இருக்குது. போர்ட்ரைட்லாம் அமோகமா இருக்குது. இருக்கும். ஒரே மெயின் கேமரா சென்சார் தான் மோஸ்ட்லி  யூஸ் பண்ணுவாங்க .  லோ லைட்ல எந்த அளவுக்கு பெர்பார்ம் பண்ணுது லோ லைட்ல எனக்கு வீடியோதான்.  சூப்பரா இருந்துச்சு .  ஆனா வேற போன கம்பேர் பண்ணும்போது லோ லைட்ல எடுக்கும்போது நாய்ச்சஸ் எல்லாம் பலியச்சுன்னு தெரியும்.  இதுல தெரியவே மாட்டிக்குது.  இந்த டீ சொ அதை பத்தி நம்ம வந்து பிக்சல்  வீடியோ பண்ணையல பேசுவோம்.  சோ ஓவரஆலா கேமரா அப்படின்னு வரையல,20,000க்கு எந்த அளவுக்கு வர்த். ஒரே ஒரு கேமராதான் கொடுத்துருக்காங்க. அது ஒன்னுதான் குறையா இருக்குது. மற்றபடி கேமரா ஓகேவான கேமராதான். இல்ல பொரிய இல்ல ஆனா கேமரா நல்ல கேமரா.  அதாவது ஒரு டிவைஸ பாக்கல ஓகே ஆனா லட்ச ,000  ரூபாய்க்கு ஏத்த கேமரா ஒரு செட்டப்பா இருக்கட்டும் ஹார்டுவேரா இருக்கட்டும் இல்ல பீச்சர்ஸ அதான் உங்க முதல் மனக்குமுறல். ஆமா ஆமா ஆமா. That being said, my 17 Pro Max still cannot survive me through a full day of travel. One day, I took it off the charger at 5:30 a.m. I used it non-stop. I was also hotspotting to my MacBook, and by 9:30 a.m., it was at 6% already. And yeah, iOS 26 still has its quirks. I've had a few freezes, even a few random reboots. It's not quite as bad as on the 16 Pro Max, but yeah, the issues are still there. So, all in all, I am quite happy that there are more upgrades to the iPhone 17 Pro Max than what I expected, such as the overall speed and fluidity, the extra display brightness, and the surprise camera processing improvements. But, I was hoping that the new telephoto, which is really the main hardware upgrade to the 17 Pro Max, would be a bigger improvement. However, it is sadly not. But with all of that being said, let me know what do you guys think of the iPhone 17 Pro Max. Stay tuned for my full iPhone Air review. That's going to be a very interesting one. And check out our Apple wallpapers for some amazing designs such as this one. I'm Daniel Tech and I'll see you guys in the next one. Zof Tech signing out. Cheers. [Music]

title: Upgrading from iPhone 15 Pro to iPhone 17 – Worth It or Not?
transcript: I've been really happy with my iPhone 15 Pro for the past two years. It's small, it's compact, it's powerful. More importantly, it is the first generation iPhone with the USB type-C port. It helps me unify all my devices cable. However, in the recent days, I found a big problem of this phone is the battery is deteriorating. Even though I checked the system, it still has more than 95% of battery health. I do notice significant less battery life when I use this phone day by day. It might be two reasons. One is the battery itself is deteriorating even though it says it's like 95% of battery health. The second reason more probably is that over the time over the two years the apps have been like evolving upgrading and they're consuming ever so slightly more energy that reduce the battery life of your day-to-day use for the phone. So after some consideration I've upgraded to the iPhone 17 the base model from my iPhone 15 Pro. So, if you also have a like two generation older phone, especially the pre pro series models, I'll share my experiences of upgrading to the iPhone 17 base model to see the advantages and drawbacks to see if it's truly worth the upgrade to iPhone 17. The first thing, let's talk about the screen. The iPhone 17 base model and as well as the iPhone 17 Pro Nonmax has 6.3 in screen. It's slightly larger than the 15 Pro's uh 6.1 in screen. holding a hand, I can definitely feel the slight difference that this is a larger phone. This is a larger phone holding in hand. Even though on paper, the screen does not feel that much of difference. In iPhone 17, they claimed adding a anti-glare coating layer uh compared to iPhone 16 series, of course, 15 series, but it's not very obvious. Maybe it's slightly better to handle the reflection and glare, but it's not very noticeable compared to iPhone 15 Pro. In terms of speed and performance between the iPhone 15 Pro versus iPhone 17, I don't really feel the differences when they're like running the same apps. Maybe the A19 chip is slightly faster, but Apple has always been doing a great job like optimizing the performance on the apps. It never feels laggy for me when using the iPhone 15 Pro. So, if you're upgrading the phone for a better performance, you won't notice a significant difference in most of the cases. And the weight difference, the iPhone 17 is 10 grams lighter than the iPhone 15 Pro. I mean, the iPhone 15 Pro is not a heavy phone in modern like smartphone standard. It weighs about 189 g compared to iPhone 17's 178 g. And because the iPhone 17 has a slightly larger screen, holding hand doesn't make me feel this is a lighter phone compared to iPhone 15 Pro. Okay, the thing most importantly for me is the battery life. I can tell the iPhone 17 has a noticeable significantly better battery life compared with the iPhone 15 Pro. Either it's on paper it has a larger battery inside or it's like real everyday usage. I have the exact the same apps and accounts running on the two phones and this one lasts longer in my day-to-day use for sure. The drawback of upgrading to iPhone 17 of course is the lacking of telephoto length. You may argue that the main sensors on the iPhone 17, like the main camera and ultra wide camera is slightly better than the iPhone 15 Pro, but I do see some YouTubers like running some blind test between the two phones cameras that uh photo shot by iPhone 15 Pro versus iPhone 17. And it's not that obvious that the iPhone 17's photo is that much better than iPhone 15 Pro. In the blind test, people actually sometimes prefer the photos from the iPhone 15 Pro, sometimes prefer it from the iPhone 17. So basically, they're the same quality, high quality of like main sensor and ultra wide sensor. However, liking of the telephoto lens is a issue for me. I know in 90% of time we're not using a phone for the cameras and even the 10% of time we're using the cameras. In 90% of time, we're not using the telephoto lens. But only for that 1% of the time. If you really need to like shoot something far away from you, then you want the telephoto lens to be available in the pocket that you can use. And lacking of that makes me feel a little bit insecure psychologically. I know for most of us it it's not an issue. It doesn't matter at all. But for me, even that 1% of cases, I do wish I have the telephoto lens. Uh they're available in my pocket just in case I need it. The front-facing camera though is a big upgrade. Now you can use the front-facing cameras horizontally or vertically holding the phone. No differences. That's really great because it's uh definitely easier to hold in hand uh with the phone like vertically in your hand. Also, the dual camera shooting support is great for vloggers. I know many people don't think it's useful, but in those cases, you want to like shoot with your back camera as well as like shooting the reactions of yourself holding this phone. either it's like a outdoor uh vlog style or a interview style videos that's really helpful. So conclusion, if you're upgrading from your iPhone 15 Pro for the sake of performance, especially CPU performance or overall performance, it doesn't really feel it worth it. I don't feel the two phones perform differently on day-to-day basis. And if you upgrade the phone for the longevity of the battery life, it does feel significantly better. and the battery life is significantly improved over the two generations. However, the drawback is also pretty obvious is the lacking of a actual camera, a lens. The telephoto lens on the iPhone 15 Pro really comes handy when you want to shoot something some object like far away from you. Even though that's not like a euro cases, you still want at least I still want that to be available when I need it. Again, for most of us, I think the iPhone 17 base this year is a great phone. I can't emphasize enough that this this is the first year that iPhone series that has upgraded the screen to the same as the Pro series that it has the promotion fast refresh screen. It also has the same thin bezels as the Pro series and the performance difference between A19 and A19 Pro in this this year iPhone 17 series are really small. So if you don't really care about the telephoto lens, the actual lens on the Pearl series, the iPhone 17 base model this year is a good upgrade.

title: IPHONE 17 NARANJAS se están VOLVIENDO ROSAS! #Shorts #Apple #iPhone #iPhone17 #Noticias
transcript: The iPhone 17 is changing color. Several users are upset because their orange iPhone is turning pink. A strange phenomenon goes mega viral in the United States.  Let me explain a little about what's happening.  As you can see, various media outlets were covering this.  The issue is that on both TikTok and X, several people were reporting that when their phone is left without a case, it's starting to lose its color due to prolonged exposure to sunlight .  There's a lot of talk about oxidation or the type of material they used to coat the phone.  There are content creators, especially those who review technology, who are leaving their phones in the sun all day to see if this is real or just another internet myth.  Another possible theory, and please see this.  Possible skin oils, sweat, cleaning products, skincare, some type of material found in certain cases. Reports of this metallic coating are starting to go viral.  Some users are very upset because they just bought the phone, but others are happy because they say they prefer a pink phone to that orange color that they ended up not liking.  I don't have the iPhone 17 Pro Max.  Has anyone here bought this model specifically in this color?  Is it true that it's turning pink?  Let us know in the comments.

title: Я не рада iPhone 17 pro max
transcript: My friend comes to me and sees that I have this brand new phone on my desk, and she's like: "Oh, wow, that's so cool, when did you buy it?"  I say: “Listen, to be honest, I didn’t buy this, Vanya gave it to me.”  And she's like, "Oh, wow, that's so cool. Are you happy to get the phone?"  No, I'm not happy about the phone. And now, girls, there will be a sincere confession.  I am actually quite cold towards material things.  They never held any particular value for me. In general, if something breaks or gets damaged, I can get over it very easily. What am I really happy about?  Not the phone, but I'm happy with the kind of man I have.  I did n't ask for this phone, girls. The only thing he asked me when they first came out was: "What color do you like?"  Vanya's attitude towards me. This is what I am really happy about. Things are not as valuable as what is behind them. The phone will now be my favorite, of course.  M.

title: iPhone 17 Pro Max (eSIM & SIM) Charging Test vs iPhone Air & 16 Pro Max – Shocking Results!
transcript: battery charging test between the iPhone 17  Pro Max SIM version, the iPhone 17 Pro Max   eim version, the iPhone 16 Pro Max and the iPhone  Air. In this video, we're going to find out which   iPhone has the fastest charging speed. Now, the  iPhone 17 Pro Max eim version has the biggest   battery on the table. It has a 5,88 mAh battery,  followed by the iPhone 17 Pro Max SIM version,   which comes with a 4,823 mAh battery. We also  have the iPhone 16 Pro Max with a 4,685 mAh   battery and the slimmest smartphone on the table,  the iPhone Air. It has a 3,149 mAh battery. Now,   for this charging test, I used a 240 W USB type-C  cable. This cable delivers 6 amp, so that's more   than enough power for all the smartphones. I also  used a USB digital tester to see the wattage it   smartphone is pulling in. All smartphones were  connected to this Oro charger. It's a 65 watts   charger. Now, the reason I use this charger is  because the iPhone 17 Pro Max pulls in 36 watts,   and most iPhone chargers in the market max out  at 30 watts. So, with an older iPhone charger,   you're not getting the fastest charging speed if  you use them. This Oro charger, on the other hand,   would give the iPhone 17 Pro Max the best charging  speed. And funny enough, it's the most affordable   65 W charger in my region. If you want to get one,  the link will be in the description of this video.   So, yeah, here we go. The iPhone Air is pulling  in a bit above 18 watts. The iPhone 16 Pro Max is   drawing in around 27 watt. The iPhone 17 Pro Max  Eim version is pulling in 38 watt and the physical   SIM version is pulling in 36 watt. If you own  the iPhone 17 Pro or Pro Max, you need at least   a 40 W fast charger. Now, I'm sure some of you are  wondering if it's safe to use a 65 watt charger on   a smartphone that pulls in 20 or 30 watts. The  short answer is yes, it's safe. The smartphone   will only pull in what it's capable of taking in.  After 6 minutes of charging, the iPhone Air is at   13%. The iPhone 16 Pro Max is at 14%. The iPhone  17 Pro Max e SIM is at 18% while the iPhone 17   Pro Max physical SIM version is at 19%. After 10  minutes of charging, the iPhone Air is at 20%. The   iPhone 16 Pro Max is at 21%. The iPhone 17 Pro  Max eim version is at 27% and the iPhone 17 Pro   Max SIM version is at 28%. Now, it's interesting  to note that charging at 40 W has been around on   Android for years. And right now, we have Android  smartphones that charge at 100 W, 120 watt, 240   watt. So, it's good to see that Apple is adopting  fast charging. At the 15 minute mark, the iPhone   Air is at 30%. The iPhone 16 Pro Max is also at  30%. The iPhone 17 Pro Max EIM is at 37% and the   iPhone 17 Pro Max same version is at 38%. At the  30 minutes mark, all smartphones are above 50%.   The iPhone Air is at 54%. The iPhone 16 Pro Max  is at 56%. The iPhone 17 Pro Max eim version is at   61% and the 17 Pro Max SIM version is at 64%. The  reason why the iPhone 17 Pro Max with the physical   SIM is ahead is because it has a smaller battery  compared to the eim version. If you compare the   charging percentage of the Pro Max version, the  17 Pro Max version and the iPhone Air and the 16   Pro Max, the difference isn't that much. After 50  minutes of charging, the iPhone Air is at 76%. The   iPhone 16 Pro Max is at 77%. The iPhone 17 Pro Max  eim and physical SIM version are both at 80%. As   you can see, the slower charging smartphones have  closed up the gap in terms of battery percentage.   Now, by default, your iPhone will charge slowly  when it gets to 80%. Now, to disable this, you'll   need to go into the settings, go to battery,  then turn off optimize battery charging and clean   energy charging if your device has that option.  The 17 Pro Max with the physical SIM version   doesn't have the clean energy charging. Now, if  you have optimized battery charging turned on,   your iPhone pes charging at 80% and finishes just  before you typically unplug it, helping to reduce   battery aging and to extend its battery life. But  turn it off, that won't matter. It's just going to   charge 1 hour into charging. The iPhone Air is at  82%. The iPhone 16 Pro Max is at 80%. The iPhone   17 Pro Max eim version is at 84%. and the iPhone  17 Pro Max physical SIM version is at 84% as well.   As you can see, once iPhones reach 80%, charging  becomes slow. Now, I would advise you to take off   the charger and start using the smartphone at  this point because it's not worth waiting for   your phone to get to 100%. After 1 hour 30 minutes  of charging, the iPhone Air is at 98%. The iPhone   16 Pro is at 93%. The iPhone 17 Pro Max eim  version is at 97% and the iPhone 17 Pro Max SIM   version is at 94%. After 1 hour 34 minutes, the  iPhone Air completes it charge. A minute later,   the iPhone 17 Pro Max eim version completes it  charge. 1 hour 56 minutes, the iPhone 17 Pro Max   physical SIM version gets to 100% and 3 minutes  later, the iPhone 16 Pro Max finishes up. Now,   here's the breakdown of the charging time for  each smartphone. The major takeaway for me is   that if you own the 17 Pro or the Pro Max, get a  40 watt or 60 W charger. Another major takeaway   is as soon as your battery gets to 80%, take off  the charger and start using the smartphone. It's   not worth waiting an additional 30 minutes for  your phone to get to 100%. Except you're charging   your smartphone overnight and that's okay. title: El test de batería de los iPhone 17 te va a sorprender
transcript: The new iPhone 17 has just had a battery test and the results will surprise you.  After the launch, Apple made it clear that the Air's battery would last very little time, because it offered a power bank along with it and promised that the 17 Pro Max would have the longest- lasting battery ever, and even more so in its EI version.  Content creator Mr. Hood of Voz has tested the battery life of the 17 series by comparing it to that of the 16 and even the S25 Ultra.  But as expected, with the 17 Pro Max still having 40% battery left, the N finished discharging.  But this is not the only unexpected result.  But before I show it to you, and speaking of external batteries that are a great fit for the iPhone Air, I can recommend this one from Chuktech because it's cheaper than Apple's and has nothing to envy in terms of stability, charging speed, and functionality, as it includes its own charging cable.  But the most impressive thing about the test is that the iPhone 17 and the 16 have ended up practically the same, so I would n't say there has been an improvement, at least not with this test.  But what has impressed me most is the battery life of the iPhone 17 Pro Max with 13 hours of battery life, being in its version and without 13 hours 45 minutes, at least 10% above the S25 Ultra.  What do you think?

title: Xiaomi 17 Pro vs. iPhone 17 Pro, Galaxy S25, Pixel 10 Pro: Best Compact Phone?
transcript: ﻿What is the best compact phone you can get right  now? The Xiaomi 17 Pro with the dual display,   the brand new orange iPhone 17 Pro, the Pixel 10  Pro with its classic Pixel look, or the Galaxy   S25, which is the lightest phone in the batch. I  bought all four of these phones with my own money,   and the Xiaomi is the Chinese version that  I bought from TradingShenzhen. I’ll put the   links in the description. Starting with the main  camera, the Xiaomi, the iPhone, and the Pixel all   have a large flagship sensor, while the S25 has  a smaller one. But you should note the S25 is   also usually cheaper in price. In these photos  of Maria with the main lens, you can see each   phone has a slightly different color science — the  Galaxy has a warmer tone, the iPhone is neutral,   while the Xiaomi and Pixel have a magenta tint.  With two-times zoom, the color shifts a bit. Here,   the Galaxy and iPhone stay neutral, Xiaomi this  time has a warmer tone, and Pixel is magenta.   I’m shooting on Leica Vibrant if you need to  know. For indoor shots with the main lens, you   can already see the smaller sensor of the Galaxy  makes the photo look not as sharp. The other three   phones are doing a great job, but the processing  is different. For portrait mode, I find all phones   do an excellent job, and the bokeh looks very  natural. The only exception is the Pixel, which   not only oversharpens the image but also makes  Maria’s hair unnaturally cut. For the ultrawide   shot, I find Xiaomi is the only phone that is  correcting the photo, and especially on the iPhone   and Pixel, Maria’s proportions look unnatural. For  the telephoto lens, the Galaxy is 3x, the iPhone   is 4x, and the other two are 5x. Of course,  that doesn’t stop you from zooming further,   but anything more than what I mentioned will  simply make the phone do a digital crop. Also,   the Galaxy has the smallest sensor size, while  the Xiaomi has the largest. That means on the   Galaxy you get the least amount of background  blur, while on the Xiaomi you get the most.   That makes Xiaomi’s telephoto portrait shots look  really professional, and Maria, as a professional   photographer, was in love with them. For zoom  photos, the Galaxy has the least reach. Also,   the Pixel and Xiaomi use Gen AI to reconstruct the  image, as you can see in this picture. I took this   photo in the basement with zero light, and you  can already see that the Galaxy cannot keep up   because of the smaller sensor, while the other  three are doing a decent job. Xiaomi nailed the   colors the best. For five-times zoom pictures, the  Galaxy is again not doing great here, and I find   the Pixel photo amazing. Xiaomi is also great, but  Maria’s shirt was green, not brown. The Galaxy is   also the only one that cannot do macro shots, but  its main lens has an acceptable minimum distance.   For selfies, the Galaxy and Xiaomi are not as  wide as I want them to be, and here I prefer the   iPhone and Pixel’s wider lenses. But the Xiaomi  17 Pro has an amazing feature, which is the second   display on the back of the phone that you can use  to take selfies with the main lens. And of course,   if you do this, the quality of the picture is  going to be head and shoulders above any selfie   camera you get on any smartphone. The iPhone also  has an advantage: the selfie sensor is square,   so you can take horizontal videos  and photos, as I’m doing right now.   For night photos, I do find the iPhone processing  super nice. The Xiaomi and Galaxy could use more   denoising, and the Pixel did too much denoising  — the stars are not visible anymore. For video,   the color science is very different between  them. The Galaxy is darker, the iPhone exposes   the video a bit better but colors are muted. I  find Xiaomi the most pleasing one here, and the   Pixel has a lot of magenta in the white balance —  I wonder why Google keeps doing this. For videos   in complete darkness, the smaller sensor size  of the Galaxy is not working to its advantage,   and I find the Pixel smoothing Maria’s skin a  bit too much. We can’t talk about compact phones   without mentioning their weight. Between the  four phones, the Galaxy is the lightest by a big   margin, and the Pixel is the heaviest. Also, the  Galaxy is the only phone with a 6.2-inch display,   while the other three are 6.3 inches. All of  them have a 120Hz display, as you would expect   from a phone these days. iPhone is the only one  with anti reflective coating. For PWM, which can   cause eye strain if it’s too low, the Galaxy is  480Hz. The Pixel also added an option to increase   it to 480Hz, but that option is turned off by  default. The iPhone has a PWM smoothing toggle,   which can help tremendously, and Xiaomi too has  an adaptive refresh rate pro option, which also   helps a lot. If you are PWM sensitive, I would  recommend the iPhone or Xiaomi. For performance,   the Galaxy is the oldest phone on the table and  it’s sporting last year’s Snapdragon 8 Elite. The   iPhone is running on Apple’s A19 Pro, the Xiaomi  is sporting the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5, and the   Pixel is running on Tensor G5. On Geekbench, for  multidiudi-core, the Xiaomi gets the best score,   followed by the iPhone, Galaxy, and Pixel. For  single-core, the iPhone gets the better score,   followed by Xiaomi, Galaxy, and finally Pixel. I  also ran 3DMark Wildlife Extreme, but the result   was rather peculiar. The Galaxy got the highest  score here — strange — followed by Xiaomi, iPhone,   and then Pixel, which is not that strange. The  Galaxy, Xiaomi, and Pixel are all running Android   16, while the iPhone is running iOS 26. The look  and feel of Xiaomi’s UI is very, very similar to   iOS sometimes, but since iOS has this new liquid  glass design now, they look slightly different.   For example, when playing music, the Galaxy has  this small bar on the top corner, while the iPhone   and Xiaomi both have a Dynamic Island. The Pixel  has the classic look still, and you have to go to   Quick Settings or the notification bar to access  playing music. Another thing that looks almost   identical on Galaxy, iPhone, and Xiaomi is  the widgets. The Pixel one looks different,   though. Even when opening the weather app,  the first three phones have a very similar UI,   while the Pixel is the only one with a different  design. I also decided to do a battery drain test   on the phones. The Xiaomi has the highest battery  capacity here, and the iPhone is the nano SIM card   version, so it has the lowest capacity. So it’s  not a surprise that the Xiaomi has the highest   battery percentage after the first part of the  battery test, which was taking photos and videos.   All phones are connected to the same 5G network,  and Wi-Fi is off. The brightness is also set the   same at 600 nits using a lux meter. I played  YouTube for an hour, and by the end of it,   the Xiaomi had the highest battery percentage at  75%, while the Galaxy had the lowest at 61%. Then   I played Google Meet on 5G again for another hour,  and Xiaomi was still top of the game, followed by   Pixel, iPhone, and Galaxy. I then removed the SIM  cards from the phones and ran the rest of the test   on Wi-Fi only. I know there are always a lot of  comments on software optimization and software   versions, but I can tell you there are two things  that are the most important factors for battery   life: the kernel your phone is running and the  hardware, with the battery and chipset being the   most important parts there. Android phones are  generally less efficient, and the reason is very   simple — if you want to make an operating system  that works for every single brand out there, you   can’t run your kernel as effectively as you want,  and there’s no way around it. With optimization,   you can only make it marginally better. So the  only way for Android phones to beat the iPhone   is through these crazy high-capacity batteries,  like what Xiaomi and other Chinese phones are   doing right now. It does seem like the Galaxy is  going to be the first phone to leave the chat.   It still did an impressive job for having a  small battery capacity for an Android phone,   in my opinion, and it’s not that far off  from the iPhone Pro and Pixel 10 Pro,   which have much larger batteries. After 7 hours  and 22 minutes, the Galaxy was the first phone to   leave the chat. The Pixel got really close to the  iPhone, and for a moment, it looked like maybe the   iPhone would even beat it. But the iPhone died  first at 8 hours and 2 minutes, while the Pixel   still had 3 percent. After a few minutes, the  Pixel left the chat at 8 hours and 11 minutes,   while the Xiaomi still had 19% left — crazy!  The Xiaomi ran for more than another hour and   left the chat at 9 hours and 28 minutes, which  is super impressive for a sub-200-gram compact   phone. This is almost iPhone 17 Pro Max territory.  Also, the Xiaomi 17 Pro has the highest charging   speed of 100W, and at least on the version  I bought from TradingShenzhen, the charger,   case, and screen protector are all included.  Overall, I would pick the Galaxy if you want   to save money and want to get the most compact  phone out there. But note that the cameras on this   phone are not flagship level, and the battery  is not going to be as good as the rest of the   pack. The iPhone is a simple and straightforward  recommendation — the battery life is a bit better,   you get flagship-level cameras, and the whole iOS  ecosystem and connection to other Apple devices.   The Xiaomi 17 Pro is a monster. The battery life  is amazing, I am in love with the telephoto lens,   and the second display is a game-changer for  selfies and content creation. The only problem   is that right now it hasn’t reached the global  market. The Pixel 10 Pro is a good choice if you   want an Android phone that is not Chinese, has  a better camera and battery life than the Galaxy   S25, and you want to have a more vanilla Android  experience. Also, Apple, Google, and Xiaomi make   cheaper versions of these models too, and if you  want to see how they compare to the Galaxy S25,   you can watch this video next. Thanks for tuning  into Techmo, and I’ll see you in the next one!

title: iPhone 17 Pro Max vs Android: Nach 4 Wochen ist klar, wer vorne liegt!
transcript: This is the iPhone 17 Pro Max and after four weeks of everyday use I have to honestly say, the hype is over.  Yes, this thing is brutally fast, the camera is powerful, but in direct comparison with a Pixel 10 Pro XL, the S25 Ultra and the Xiaomi 15 Ultra, one thing strikes me .  Apple is still playing at the top, but no longer at the very top. Welcome back.  It's great that you're back again .  When the iPhone 17 Pro Max was introduced, it was an absolute hype monster.  And yes, I played my part and I confess to being guilty, but to be quite honest, when this thing comes out, so much has always changed, at least at first glance, that you can't help but be thrilled.  There was a new design, a new telephoto camera, completely new cooling; everything sounded like a huge step forward.  I 've been using it in everyday life for four weeks now, taking photos, playing games, filming, and repeatedly comparing it to the S25 Ultra, the Xiaomi 15 Ultra, or even the Pixel 10 Pro XL, and honestly, Apple delivers, but Android has caught up and in some aspects even surpassed it, and that's exactly what we're going to take a closer look at now .  Apple has said goodbye to titanium and is now introducing a unibody design made of aluminum.  Sounds like progress, but somehow it feels a little different, at least to me in reality.  The iPhone feels rounder in the hand, but somehow quickly becomes dull, scratches more easily, and the titanium feel is gone.  One could of course say, okay, most of the time you use a case anyway, but I'm also one of those people who keep taking the case off because the devices are somehow so ingenious, and that's when I notice it .  The new wave chamber cooling keeps performance stable, but at the same time, the case somehow seems less professional as a result.  And when I put my S25 Ultra next to it, the S25 Ultra is simply more robust and ultimately seems higher quality to me.  I have the Jet version and it stays just as cool and delivers exactly the same performance, so Samsung makes the whole thing a bit quieter compared to Apple, but does at least the same job.  So, for me, that doesn't automatically mean that this exchange is a good thing, because while the technology has won, the iPhone has unfortunately lost character.  And before we launch into the classic Apple bashing, let me know in the comments: am I being too harsh on the iPhone 17 Pro Max, or is this ultimately the reality?  Write it in my comments and please subscribe to my channel if you haven't already . By the way, www.smartobblogger.de offers free podcasts and free newsletters.   So secure your spot now.  I'd be happy if you joined us. Press the hype button and let's continue .  And now let's move on to the display, because Apple remains strong here.  120 Hz Promotion, 3000 nit brightness, magnificent colors.  And I must say, I really like that.  They've always done a great job.  And now there's one more thing.  The iPhone 17, the base iPhone that he can get for €1000 , has exactly the same panel. And after four weeks, I really have to say that I feel a bit ripped off for €1400 , because a basic device has exactly the same display as a Pro Display. Well, where exactly are the differences then?  And that's what I think is a bit of a problem for me, because then the camera comes into play, because this new quad lens is really powerful, especially for portraits, and I've already made that a bit clear in one or two shorts , but at night I still have noise and focus problems.  And if I then take, for example, the Xiaomi 15 Ultra with its 1-inch sensor Licensing, which has a much better dynamic range, and then comes the real kicker when I add, for example, my Pixel 10 Pro XL, which, thanks to the AI ​​function, simply does a much better job. So, these are things where I just say, this shouldn't be happening, Apple, you could do better.  So, they did optimize a lot, but they didn't really make a significant leap forward .  One leap forward they have made, and they really do this year after year, and I really appreciate that , is this performance. This year it's the A19 Pro chip and that thing is an absolute machine. Triple A Games are therefore possible.  You won't have any heat problems thanks to the Waver Chamber cooling system, for example this year. But in everyday use, this simply doesn't help you at all , because the apps open just as fast as on the iPhone 16 Pro, 15 Pro, 14 Pro, 13 Pro and seemingly all other iPhones before them.  And yes, one could say, yes, other smartphones have that too, that's true , but they position the whole thing a little differently.  And now comes the crux of the matter for me, because the Galaxy S25 Ultra with the Snapdragon processor simply outperforms it in multitasking, because you have, for example, this Dex mode.  This means you can connect the Galaxy to the monitor and then your Galaxy will be the computer at that moment. But you can also use normal split screen; it has AI features included, which is simply awesome, and the Google 10 Pro XL, for example, makes life easier for me with live translation, language tools, and smart answers .  Yes, Apple has the power, but these Android smartphones are simply much better in everyday use because they are more suited to your daily life. So they deliver what you really need in everyday life, because I think most of you do n't need that most extreme performance. And after four weeks, I simply noticed again in everyday life that I wasn't really using this A19 Pro that much .  And that's why I'm being honest now, after 4 weeks my euphoria is gone.  The iPhone 17 Pro Max is an impressive device, but it no longer inspires the same enthusiasm it did at the beginning.  Of course, one could also say that many smartphones lose some of their charm after a period of use .  That's okay, but the iPhone always positions itself as being the very best .  And the camera is really powerful, but it's no longer leading the pack at this point, for example .  The display is brilliant, but it hasn't improved, and I now get it in the basic iPhone, which is a problem for me.  The performance is still outstanding, but you hardly notice it in everyday life, because you don't need that level of performance for what you do with it .  And the case is beautiful, and I also liked how it was shown in the presentation, and I also like that they are providing a new design .  That is also extremely important.  But even there, I must say it's a bit too sensitive. Now I think that if you want an all-round phone that does n't annoy you, then get the iPhone 17 Pro, because it really covers a lot. But if you're looking for technical innovations, like a better display for example, then you have the S25 Ultra.  A camera that delivers so much, especially with Leica support, makes you feel much more comfortable with a Xiaomi 15 Ultra in 2025. And after a month of everyday use, it's clear to me that the hype surrounding the iPhone 17 Pro Max was huge, but the reality is a bit sobering.  Apple delivers this stability, but not a revolution. Android simply offers much more variety.  This willingness to experiment, to try new things and discover features that you really notice as you get older, especially with a focus on AI, makes a huge difference.  And Apple is no longer fighting for dominance, but rather for relevance.  And as harsh as it all sounds, that's how it is in the end.  Okay, admittedly, in the end it's this classic battle of Android versus iPhone or iOS.  That 's true in a certain way, but I think it's becoming increasingly clear that Apple has kind of squandered it, and not just with this design, but also with the designs before it.  Because devices like the Pixel demonstrate what's possible, that you don't need the best performance to get through everyday life well, and that you do n't need the most amazing lenses to take the best photos, because the magic word in the end is AI, whether positive or negative, but this is changing our lives and not embracing it and still sticking to your old ways is a bit difficult.  Perhaps it's a stretch to delve so deeply into this after just one month and say that AI will be the key for Apple too, but after a month the hype has died down a bit for me and I'm going back to my Android devices, which provide so much added value in my everyday life, instead of just having an Apple logo on the back.  Thank you so much for being part of this video. Stay healthy, take care, see you soon.  Your smartphone blogger.

So, not only did they skip the wide-angle lens , they also skipped the main camera. less wide.  Here we have a normal main module of 24 mm.  There is a 0.5 camera, also of good quality. Seriously, I don't want to find fault with this module in this phone , when in Pros, for $1,000 or $200 or more, I would like to get better quality in low light.  Televк to some extent replaces the 2x from the main camera, so I can’t say that it’s really missing here.  Although in 17 Pro it has become much better than in 16 Pro.  I hardly ever use it here because the quality is either terrible or the iPhone won't switch to it at all in poor lighting.  The battery is also better than the 16, but not as good as the 17 Pro Max. This year we still have more than 5,000, which is enough for a day and a half of use.  Here it’s somewhere around 3,700, considering that the display is not that big and power-hungry, although very bright, again, I think it will still be enough for your phone for about a day .  Even the iPhone Air, with its tiny battery, after a few days of use, after all the system optimization, starts to last people almost a whole day. Things aren't as bad after 2 months as many expected.  And when that battery runs out, you'll be able to charge it much faster than before.  Apple promises that you'll get about 50% charge from a 40W power supply in 20 minutes .  We couldn't even dream of this before.  And let's be honest, even if your Android phone came with a 100W power adapter, it's unlikely to charge your phone much faster than this iPhone charges.  Therefore, the idea that iPhones have insanely slow charging is definitely a myth after the iPhone 17.  Maavey charging , by the way, has also become faster.  Now the max save is 25W, but this update is now also available on the 16-inch models.  If any of you use an iPhone 16, please let me know in the comments if it charges faster.  In conclusion, what I want to say about this phone.  This is how the iPhone should have come out without huge 120Hz bezels .  The only thing is that no one asked for a square front camera last year.  It is very nice to receive such unexpected but pleasant treats.  Yes, we only have two cameras at the back instead of three, but as we've discovered, not everyone needs a third .  At the same time, there is not one camera here, like in the iPhone, which is a pain, and there are no mono speakers.  There are normal, very high-quality stereo speakers here.  Again, a perfect super-bright screen, normal performance and, unlike the iPhone Air, a normal battery that will last you for one day.  It's similar to the iPhone 11 not only in color, but also in that, just like the 11, it covers the needs of most users who don't need to play detrending games on their phone, who don't need a telescope in their phone, or who, for example, don't constantly transfer terabytes of data from their phone to a computer via USBC, because it remains as slow as before.  I think that in a few years, especially when this phone becomes cheaper, and even more so considering that Apple, most likely, according to rumors, will not show the iPhone 18 next September, but will postpone it until the spring.   With all that in mind, I think this will be an insanely popular iPhone that will be relevant for years to come, just like the iPhone 11 that many people are still using.  Even I wasn't bothered by any of his minuses .  And if I hadn't bought the iPhone 16 Pro Max last year and was now looking for a replacement for the iPhone 13, I definitely wouldn't have overpaid and would have bought it from Technodeus with a discount using the promo code tech, a warranty, and delivery to anywhere in Russia and Belarus.  Well, come to our cozy showroom.  I'm giving this phone to our operator because I hope I've now convinced him that it's a decent device.  I hope you too.  Bye. Thank you. Damn, he has two cameras. Why iPhone 17?
